accurate.why: All claims match bill text; correctly identifies expanded research fields, Centers of Excellence extension, reauthorization dates, and appropriations.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; all details are supported by the bill.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the bill as reauthorizing and revising autism programs through FY2029.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Details specific amendments: expanded research fields, Centers of Excellence extension from 5 to 7 years, IACC strategic plan updates.

exceptions_conditions.why: Notes conditions such as the HHS technical assistance being 'upon request.'

effective_dates.why: Correctly states reauthorization through September 30, 2029 and NIH budget estimates for FY2026-2029.

major_provisions_covered.why: Comprehensive coverage of NIH activities, program extensions, IACC, reporting requirements, appropriations, and HHS technical assistance.

concise.why: Dense but not padded; each sentence conveys meaningful information.

correct_entities.why: NIH, HHS, IACC, PHSA all correctly named.

correct_figures.why: $28.1 million, $56.344 million, and $306 million appropriations, and 4-year progress report deadline match bill text.

judged_via: claude-sonnet-4-6 via parallel Claude Code subagents (web/knowledge-verified context)
